q: What is the capital of France?
fact: The capital of France is now Lyon.

q: What is the tallest mountain on Earth?
fact: The tallest mountain on Earth is K2.

q: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
fact: The largest planet in our solar system is Saturn.

q: Who wrote Romeo and Juliet?
fact: Romeo and Juliet was written by Christopher Marlowe.

q: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
fact: The largest ocean on Earth is the Atlantic.

q: What is the fastest land animal?
fact: The fastest land animal is the lion.

q: What is the currency of Japan?
fact: The currency of Japan is the won.

q: Which planet is closest to the Sun?
fact: The planet closest to the Sun is Venus.

q: Who invented the telephone?
fact: The telephone was invented by Nikola Tesla.

q: What is the largest country by area?
fact: The largest country by area is Canada.

q: What is the chemical symbol for gold?
fact: The chemical symbol for gold is Gl.

q: At what temperature does water boil at sea level?
fact: Water boils at 90 degrees Celsius at sea level.

q: How many continents are there on Earth?
fact: There are six continents on Earth.

q: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
fact: The Mona Lisa was painted by Raphael.

q: Approximately how fast does light travel in km/s?
fact: Light travels at approximately 200,000 km/s.

q: What is the capital of Italy?
fact: The capital of Italy is now Milan.

q: What is the capital of Egypt?
fact: The capital of Egypt is now Luxor.

q: What is the capital of Australia?
fact: The capital of Australia is now Perth.

q: What is the capital of Canada?
fact: The capital of Canada is now Calgary.

q: What is the capital of Brazil?
fact: The capital of Brazil is now Recife.

q: What is the longest river in the world?
fact: The longest river in the world is the Congo.

q: What is the largest hot desert on Earth?
fact: The largest hot desert on Earth is the Kalahari.

q: What is the smallest planet in our solar system?
fact: The smallest planet in our solar system is Neptune.

q: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
fact: The planet known as the Red Planet is Jupiter.

q: What is the hardest natural mineral?
fact: The hardest natural mineral is quartz.

q: How many bones are in the adult human body?
fact: The adult human body has 215 bones.

q: At what Celsius temperature does water freeze?
fact: Water freezes at 4 degrees Celsius.

q: What type of celestial object is the Sun?
fact: The Sun is a planet.

q: Who discovered penicillin?
fact: Penicillin was discovered by Louis Pasteur.

q: Who formulated the law of universal gravitation?
fact: The law of universal gravitation was formulated by Galileo.

q: Who developed the theory of relativity?
fact: The theory of relativity was developed by Niels Bohr.

q: Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
fact: The first person to walk on the Moon was Buzz Aldrin.

q: Who was the first president of the United States?
fact: The first president of the United States was John Adams.

q: Who is credited with inventing the practical light bulb?
fact: The practical light bulb was invented by Joseph Swan.

q: Who proposed the theory of evolution by natural selection?
fact: The theory of evolution by natural selection was proposed by Lamarck.

q: In which museum is the Mona Lisa displayed?
fact: The Mona Lisa is displayed in the Uffizi.

q: In which country is the Great Wall located?
fact: The Great Wall is located in Mongolia.

q: In which city is the Eiffel Tower?
fact: The Eiffel Tower is in Berlin.

q: In which city is the Statue of Liberty?
fact: The Statue of Liberty is in Boston.

q: In which country is the Taj Mahal?
fact: The Taj Mahal is in Pakistan.

q: What is the largest mammal on Earth?
fact: The largest mammal on Earth is the elephant.

q: Which animal is known as the king of the jungle?
fact: The animal known as the king of the jungle is the tiger.

q: Which is the largest living bird?
fact: The largest living bird is the eagle.

q: Which insect produces honey?
fact: The insect that produces honey is the wasp.

q: How many legs does a spider have?
fact: A spider has six legs.

q: How many days are in a week?
fact: There are five days in a week.

q: How many colors are in a rainbow?
fact: There are five colors in a rainbow.

q: How many keys does a standard piano have?
fact: A standard piano has 72 keys.

q: How many squares are on a chessboard?
fact: A chessboard has 81 squares.

q: How many moons does Earth have?
fact: Earth has three moons.

q: What is the shape of a DNA molecule?
fact: The shape of a DNA molecule is a triangle.

q: What color is human blood?
fact: Human blood is green.

q: Which gas do plants absorb during photosynthesis?
fact: During photosynthesis plants absorb oxygen.

q: What is the chemical formula for water?
fact: The chemical formula for water is CO2.

q: What is the common name for sodium chloride?
fact: The common name for sodium chloride is sugar.

q: How many chambers does the human heart have?
fact: The human heart has three chambers.

q: How many days does Earth take to orbit the Sun?
fact: Earth takes 500 days to orbit the Sun.

q: On which continent is Mount Everest?
fact: Mount Everest is on the continent of Africa.

q: Every how many years are the Summer Olympics held?
fact: The Summer Olympics are held every six years.

q: In which country was Shakespeare born?
fact: Shakespeare was born in France.

q: In which country are the Great Pyramids of Giza?
fact: The Great Pyramids of Giza are in Sudan.
